name: La Sierra de la Aixortá — Circular por Guadalest
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Callosa D'En Sarrià traverse a landscape characterized by impressive mountain ranges such as Aitana, Serrella, Ponotx, and Bernia, alongside the lush valleys of the Algar and Guadalest rivers. This mountainous terrain provides a diverse network of routes, from rugged climbs to paths winding through natural parks like Fuentes del Algar. The region's limestone formations and elevated vantage points offer varied riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Callosa D'En Sarrià The most popular mountain bike trail is La Sierra de la Aixortá — Circular por Guadalest, a 26.8 miles (43.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the surrounding sierras. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Path Along the Aqueduct – Polop de la Marina loop from la Nucia, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and traverses varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Polop de la Marina – Polop - Plaza de Xorros loop from la Nucia, a 14.4 miles (23.2 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Callosa D'En Sarrià is defined by rugged mountains, river valleys, and reservoirs, with routes offering views of the inland Costa Blanca. The network provides options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Callosa D'En Sarrià are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 5000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Callosa D'En Sarrià's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: Callosa D'En Sarrià offers a vast network of mountain bike trails, with over 120 routes available. This includes a diverse range of options, from easier paths to very challenging climbs, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain biking in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: The trails around Callosa D'En Sarrià offer a good mix of difficulties. You'll find approximately 11 easy routes, 50 moderate routes, and 63 difficult routes. This means there are plenty of options for both beginners and experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: Mountain biking in Callosa D'En Sarrià takes you through diverse terrain, characterized by impressive mountain ranges like Aitana and Bernia, lush river valleys of the Algar and Guadalest, and limestone formations. You can expect rugged climbs, winding paths through natural parks, and routes offering spectacular views of the inland Costa Blanca and reservoirs.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes with scenic viewpoints in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: Yes, the region is known for its breathtaking views. Many routes traverse the surrounding sierras, offering elevated vantage points. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17319813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from la Nucia&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas of the reservoir. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cocoll&lt;/a&gt; which offer panoramic views.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by natural features like waterfalls?
A: Absolutely. The famous Fuentes del Algar (Algar Waterfalls) is a prominent natural attraction in the area. While specific mountain bike trails might not go directly through the protected waterfall area, many routes will be in the vicinity, allowing you to combine your ride with a visit to these crystal-clear waters and natural pools.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924858760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra de la Aixortá — Circular por Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 43.1 km trail that takes about 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. Another great loop is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polop de la Marina – Polop - Plaza de Xorros loop from la Nucia&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.2 km long.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. Many routes feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Top Singletrail – Coll de Rates Restaurant loop from Callosa d'en Sarrià&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 61.7 km route with over 1390 meters of elevation gain. The 'Coll de Rates' itself is a legendary climb in the Costa Blanca, popular with professional cyclists.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: The mountain bike trails in Callosa D'En Sarrià are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 5,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the spectacular scenery and diverse riding experiences.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: Callosa D'En Sarrià benefits from a favorable climate, making it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable, while winters are typically mild.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions to look out for along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the region has several points of interest. You might encounter highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Governor's Height&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4757211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morro Blau - Serra de l'Aixorta&lt;/a&gt;, which are mountain peaks offering great views. The area also has a rich history, with remnants of ancient walls and the archpriestal church of San Juan Bautista in Callosa d'En Sarrià itself.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails in Callosa D'En Sarrià?
A: While popular routes can see more traffic, the extensive network of over 120 trails means it's often possible to find quieter paths. Exploring some of the less-known moderate or difficult routes, particularly during off-peak hours or seasons, can lead to a more solitary riding experience amidst the region's rugged mountains and valleys.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Callosa D'En Sarrià offers around 11 easy mountain bike routes that are generally more suitable for families or beginners. These trails typically have less elevation gain and smoother terrain, allowing for a more relaxed riding experience while still enjoying the beautiful natural surroundings.
tips:
The trail in (on) the riverbed is absolutely doable! The MTB S0 classification is actually appropriate, and in places it changes to S1, but without any further difficulty. Partly a “cycle path”.

The car gate to the access road down (or up) into the gorge to the Rio, near the dam, is currently always open. If it is closed, there is a door for bicycles in the fence. This was broken, however, hence the open gate.

Path with beautiful views of the reservoir and in very good condition both for walking and for cycling mtb or gravel. It connects the town of Beniarda with the reservoir dam. It does not present any technical difficulties and the environment is very pleasant!
Hiking trail in good condition surrounded by trees without technical difficulty. It allows connecting with the Camí de l'Ombria and reaching the reservoir dam

A prime location, the journey is well worth it. From here, you'll enjoy magnificent views of the emerald-green Guadalest reservoir, which stretches picturesquely between the mountains. The lake is easily accessible by bike via a paved road.

Paved road with very nice views of the Guadalest reservoir and surrounded by impressive mountains. The slopes are quite steep and the physical effort is felt, but the views at every turn are comforting.
It can be done on both a mtb and gravel bike.
Beautiful site with amazing views towards Les Foies and the town of Castell de Castells. An ideal point to get some air after the physical effort involved in getting there!

Very entertaining trail for mtb that runs through a very pleasant environment. It does not present great technical difficulty and for the most part it is clean without rock. Without a doubt recommended!

At 1,125 meters above sea level, the top of Morro Blau is the second highest in the Serra de l'Aixorta chain. Unlike the Penya Blanca (the highest peak), from here you will have amazing views towards the Marina Alta, Baja and you will be able to see the beaches from Denia through Calpe to Altea. Simply wonderful!!!
